title: The Mystery of Animal Migration
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Every year, billions of animals undertake extraordinary journeys in a process known as a (0) natural behaviour. The distances covered by some migrating creatures are truly (17) fascinating. Monarch butterflies, for instance, travel thousands of miles to reach their (18) distant winter habitats. (19) Scientific research has shown that animals use a variety of internal compasses, including the Earth's magnetic field, to navigate. The (20) astonishment researchers feel when studying these abilities remains as strong as ever. Animals appear to possess an extraordinary inner (21) navigation system that cannot yet be fully explained. Climate change and habitat loss present (22) threatening conditions for many migratory species. Animals that are (23) unable to adapt to these changes may face decline or extinction. Experts warn that (24) climatic shifts could permanently disrupt migration patterns worldwide.

example.text: Every year, billions of animals undertake extraordinary journeys in a process known as a (0) NATURAL behaviour. (NATURE)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed after 'truly'; 'fascinate' becomes 'fascinating' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
An adjective is needed before 'winter habitats'; 'distance' becomes 'distant' by changing '-ce' to '-t'.
An adjective is needed before 'research'; 'science' becomes 'scientific' by adding the suffix '-ific'.
A noun is needed after 'The'; 'astonish' becomes 'astonishment' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
A noun is needed before 'system'; 'navigate' becomes 'navigation' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
An adjective is needed before 'conditions'; 'threat' → 'threaten' → 'threatening' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'un-' is added to 'able' to mean 'not capable'.
An adjective is needed before 'shifts'; 'climate' becomes 'climatic' by adding the suffix '-ic'.
